node.title: Uber Offer | SDE1, Hyderabad | June 2022 | Accepted

topic.title: Uber Offer | SDE1, Hyderabad | June 2022 | Accepted

post.content:
Hi community. Recently I appeared for Uber L3 interviews. Leetcode community has helped me a lot during the preparation, hence want to share my experience.

This was an Off-campus drive for the Uber University Program. Applications of the same come around end of April. Keep an eye on the Careers portal :)

Background:

SDE Intern at a product based startup.
B.Tech in IT from Tier 3 college

Note : Due to NDA, won't be able to tell the exact questions.

**Online Assessment:**
In OA, there are mainly 3 problems, one is a easy implementation problem and rest are problems from graph and DP.
1. Change number from base 2 to base 6
2. A variation of the burst balloons problem
3. A DFS based problems(LC medium)

Passed on to Onsites.

Interview Preparation: I am doing LC for the past one year and have solved most of the common interview problems. I have also solved the Uber tagged questions.
Also, I've done a lot of competitve programming during my college. Solved around 2000+ problems during the entire 4 years.

Onsites

**Round 1(Coding, 45 minutes):**
I was asked two questions in this round.

Question based on Graph(DFS):
The question was  a variation of number of islands problems.
Provided DFS solution. Interviewer was not satisfied and asked to optimise. Provided a DSU based solution after some thinking. Interviewer was satisfied, I coded the solution pretty quickly.

Question based on Segment Tree:
The question was a simple point update query. Explained the approach, and coded it within time.

Interview ended on time.
Verdict - Moved on to next round

**Round 2(Coding, 45 minutes):**
This round was comparatively easy than the last round.

Question involving binary search:
Was asked a pretty simple binary search problem based on sorted matrix. Coded it within a few minutes.

Question based on on Trees:
A problem similar to House Robber 3. Solved it easily. 

Round ended within 25 minutes.

Verdict : Moved on to managerial round


**Round 3(Managerial + LLD round, 90 minutes):**
This round was with the Engineering manager and I was asked mainly about the projects I did in the intership and my personal projects, in-depth. Then he moved on to and asked me to design BookMyShow app. He was looking on how I approach the problem, how I would scale system in case of billion customers etc. The round went smooth.


Post Onsites:
Got the offer in the next 15 days and was assigned in the Fintech team, based on my preferred tech stack

My 2 cents:

* Communication matters a lot: Apart from DS and coding, Thinking out loud was the main thing that stood out for me. Tell every step you are doing, so that interviewers are on the same page as yours.
* Practise : Keep a practise to solve atleast 1 problem everyday, and try to participate in the Leetcode contests. It will help a lot.
* Never give up attitude : I was rejected by Amazon earlier, but didn't give up, which eventually helped me to crack other offers.
 

Thank you for reading. If you found this post helpful, please do upvote. :)

Compensation - https://leetcode.com/discuss/compensation/2253960/Uber-or-SE-1(L3)-or-Hyderabad

Connect here : [Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/koulick-sadhu/)
